items.text: Beginning March 3, the Blue Line campus shuttle will detour around Maple Avenue construction. New temporary stops are located at Carver Hall and South Lot B. A reduced fare of $1.50 applies until March 17. Contact Transit Services with questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the fare during the detour period?
questions.explanation: The notice states a reduced fare of $1.50 applies until March 17, so $1.50 is the correct amount.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Blue Line shuttle after March 17?

options.A: It will charge passengers a higher daily deposit.
options.B: It will permanently end service to Carver Hall.
options.C: It will likely return to its regular route and fare.
options.D: It will replace South Lot B with a new station.

questions.explanation: Because the reduced fare and detour stops are described as temporary and lasting only until March 17, the shuttle is expected to resume its normal route and pricing afterward.

items.text: Notice: HVAC maintenance will be performed in Anderson Hall from June 8 to June 10. Temperatures in classrooms may rise above normal levels during these dates. Instructors are advised to relocate afternoon sessions to Room 112 in the adjacent building.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen in Anderson Hall during the maintenance?

options.A: Classrooms will be locked to all instructors.
options.B: Classrooms may become warmer than usual.
options.C: Classrooms will be cleaned every afternoon.
options.D: Classrooms will be moved to another campus.

questions.explanation: The notice warns that temperatures may rise above normal levels, meaning the rooms may become warmer than usual.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about afternoon classes during this period?

options.A: They may be more comfortable in Room 112.
options.B: They will be cancelled for three full days.
options.C: They must be rescheduled to early mornings.
options.D: They will require students to pay a fee.

questions.explanation: Instructors are advised to relocate afternoon sessions to Room 112, implying that room avoids the rising heat and would be more comfortable.

items.text: Hi Daniel, thanks for signing up. Your campus tour guide shift is confirmed for Friday, April 18, from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM. Please report to the Visitor Center desk and check in with Ms. Reyes before your tour begins. Best, Admissions Office

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Daniel do before his tour starts?

options.A: Email Ms. Reyes a copy of his schedule
options.B: Collect a name badge from the office
options.C: Pay a deposit at the Visitor Center
options.D: Check in with Ms. Reyes at the desk

questions.explanation: The email instructs Daniel to report to the Visitor Center desk and check in with Ms. Reyes before the tour begins.

questions.stem: In the email, the word "confirmed" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: "Confirmed" here means the shift is settled and certain, which matches "finalized."

items.text: Dear Priya, we received your request for a replacement student ID. A $10 fee will be charged to your account, and your new card will be ready for pickup at the Campus Card Office on Tuesday, May 6, after 2:00 PM. Regards, Card Services

questions.stem: According to the email, when can Priya pick up her new ID?
questions.explanation: The email states the card will be ready for pickup on Tuesday, May 6, after 2:00 PM.

questions.stem: In the email, the word "charged" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: "Charged to your account" means the $10 will be billed to her, so "billed" is the closest meaning.
